steps.title: Identify the axes and their meaning
steps.content: The graph plots time (in seconds) on the x-axis and height above ground (in meters) on the y-axis. Any point on this graph is written as (time, height), so the x-coordinate tells us when and the y-coordinate tells us how high the ball is at that moment.

steps.title: Read the coordinates of the marked point
steps.content: The marked point is (1.0, 4.8). This means x = 1.0 and y = 4.8. Therefore, at time = 1.0 second, the height = 4.8 meters. Translating to plain language: 1.0 second after the ball was launched, its height above ground is 4.8 meters. That matches choice A — A is correct.
